commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b716f459111da335b445300c418bc4889149ff60)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `mimo-v2.5-pro` and `mimo-v2.5` to the Xiaomi pay-as-you-go catalog, changes the pay-as-you-go default to `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro`, and updates Xiaomi docs plus catalog/onboarding tests.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +20, Docs +22. Total +70 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; source inspection confirms current main and v2026.6.10 lack the pay-as-you-go V2.5 rows, but the PR's default-choice behavior is a product/upgrade decision.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the Xiaomi pay-as-you-go default and the contributor needs real behavior proof; this should not be auto-repaired or merged from tests alone.

target.title: feat(xiaomi): expose MiMo V2.5 on pay-as-you-go and default to mimo-v2.5-pro
target.body:
Refs #87277

## What Problem This Solves

Pay-as-you-go Xiaomi users could not select Xiaomi's current model generation.
The `xiaomi` provider (pay-as-you-go, `https://api.xiaomimimo.com/v1`) only
listed the legacy MiMo V2 catalog (`mimo-v2-flash`, `mimo-v2-pro`,
`mimo-v2-omni`) and defaulted to `mimo-v2-flash`, even though the same endpoint
already serves the MiMo V2.5 generation. Only `xiaomi-token-plan` exposed V2.5.
During onboarding and model selection, pay-as-you-go users were steered to an
outdated default and could not pick `mimo-v2.5` / `mimo-v2.5-pro` at all.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds `mimo-v2.5-pro` and `mimo-v2.5` to the `xiaomi` pay-as-you-go catalog
(ordered ahead of the legacy V2 rows so they surface first in the picker) and
flips the pay-as-you-go default from `mimo-v2-flash` to `mimo-v2.5-pro`, matching
the existing Token Plan default. The default alias is now derived from the model
name, mirroring the Token Plan preset so both providers behave identically. The
legacy `mimo-v2-*` rows are kept selectable for continuity and marked superseded
in docs.

**MiMo V2.5 omni note (important scoping):** `mimo-v2.5` is natively an omni
model (text, image, audio, video). However, the bundled plugin manifest
`modelCatalog` input set is limited to `text` / `image` / `document` by
`MODEL_CATALOG_INPUTS` in `@openclaw/model-catalog-core`
(`packages/model-catalog-core/src/model-catalog-normalize.ts:32`) — `audio` and
`video` declared in a manifest row are silently dropped during normalization
(verified: the static catalog test returns `["text","image"]` when the manifest
declares all four). So this PR advertises `mimo-v2.5` as `text, image` to match
runtime reality and documents how to declare audio/video via a custom
`openclaw.json` `models` entry (the user-config path *does* accept all four via
`zod-schema.core.ts`). Advertising native omni from the bundled catalog would
require extending `MODEL_CATALOG_INPUTS` in core, which is a separate shared
change and out of scope here.

### Issue #87277 checklist status

| Item | Status |
|---|---|
| Add `mimo-v2.5` to built-in Xiaomi catalog | Done (both `xiaomi` and `xiaomi-token-plan`) |
| Add `mimo-v2.5` to onboarding model selection | Done (catalog drives the picker) |
| Update docs with V2.5 catalog row | Done (`docs/providers/xiaomi.md`) |
| Migration note: V2.5 supersedes V2-Omni | Done (docs note + default flip) |
| `input: ["text","image","audio","video"]` in catalog | Partial — blocked on `MODEL_CATALOG_INPUTS` (see note above); advertised as `text, image` |
| `multimodalFallback` config key | Out of scope (core agent-routing feature, not provider metadata) |
| Pre-dispatch input-type inspection + history injection | Out of scope (same core routing feature) |

## User Impact

- Pay-as-you-go onboarding (`openclaw onboard --auth-choice xiaomi-api-key`) now
  defaults to `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro` and offers `xiaomi/mimo-v2.5`.
- `openclaw models list --provider xiaomi` lists the V2.5 rows.
- Existing configs pinning a `mimo-v2-*` model keep working; those rows remain in
  the catalog.

## Model parameters

Pricing = Xiaomi's published overseas pay-as-you-go USD/1M tokens
(https://mimo.mi.com/docs/zh-CN/price/pay-as-you-go). Specs cross-checked against
https://mimo.mi.com/models/mimo-v2.5 , https://mimo.mi.com/models/mimo-v2.5-pro
and the live OpenRouter models API (https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models).

| Model | Input | Context | Max output | Reasoning | input $ | output $ | cacheRead $ |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `mimo-v2.5-pro` | text | 1,048,576 | 131,072 | Yes | 0.435 | 0.87 | 0.0036 |
| `mimo-v2.5` | text, image | 1,048,576 | 131,072 | Yes | 0.14 | 0.28 | 0.0028 |

Pay-as-you-go has no context-length tiered pricing (unlike Token Plan), so no
`tieredPricing` is set. `mimo-v2.5-pro` is text-only on OpenRouter and Xiaomi;
its `0.435/0.87/0.0036` rates match OpenRouter exactly.

## Evidence

`pnpm test extensions/xiaomi` — 40 passed (3 files):

```
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

`extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts` asserts the exact pay-as-you-go catalog order
`[mimo-v2.5-pro, mimo-v2.5, mimo-v2-flash, mimo-v2-pro, mimo-v2-omni]`, the
reasoning flags, and that `mimo-v2.5` resolves to `["text","image"]` from the
manifest (documenting the `MODEL_CATALOG_INPUTS` constraint).
`extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts` asserts the new default ref
`xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro` and the `Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro` alias.
target.author: idootop

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
